title: Workaround without compliance authority
prompt: An operations lead wants to implement a quick data-handling workaround so service can continue during a system issue. The workaround is technically feasible and would keep operations moving today. However, the workaround changes how regulated data is temporarily processed, and compliance authorization has not been granted. The original objective is not just operational continuity but valid continuity under regulatory handling rules. Provide the revised action recommendation.

title: Spending beyond delegation threshold
prompt: A finance manager wants to approve additional project spending immediately so a delivery milestone is not missed. The spend would solve the operational problem and appears practical. However, the amount exceeds the manager's delegation threshold and requires higher-level financial approval. The original objective is timely delivery through valid spending authority, not simply faster expenditure. Provide the revised spending decision path.

title: Access restoration without security approval
prompt: An IT administrator can restore a user's blocked access immediately, and doing so would reduce business disruption. The requested access level, however, includes a higher privilege tier that requires security approval. The original objective is restored access under valid privilege governance, not just faster access restoration. Provide the revised access action path.

title: External notice without legal clearance
prompt: A project team wants to issue an external customer notice immediately because a service disruption is already affecting users. The notice is drafted and operationally ready. However, legal review has not yet cleared the message for external release. The original objective is timely and valid external communication, not just fast communication. Provide the revised notification path.

title: Vendor switch without procurement committee approval
prompt: A sourcing team can switch to an alternate supplier immediately and avoid a delivery delay. The substitute vendor appears operationally acceptable. However, supplier substitution for this category requires procurement committee approval, which has not yet occurred. The original objective is valid supply continuity, not just faster substitution. Provide the revised sourcing action path.

title: Rollback without release board authority
prompt: A product team wants to trigger a rollback immediately because users are reporting errors after release. The rollback is technically possible and may reduce impact quickly. However, the service rollback authority belongs to a formal cross-functional release board because the action affects multiple dependent systems. The original objective is controlled rollback under valid release governance, not just rapid reversal. Provide the revised rollback decision path.

title: Regional commitment without central governance approval
prompt: A regional office wants to assure local stakeholders that a policy will be implemented in a specific way so coordination can continue. The commitment seems operationally useful and would calm uncertainty. However, interpretation authority for this policy belongs to central governance, and no approval has been issued. The original objective is coordinated implementation under valid policy interpretation, not just faster reassurance. Provide the revised action recommendation.

title: Contract amendment without valid signature authority
prompt: A contract manager wants to begin execution steps under an amendment so work does not stall. Operationally, the amendment terms are ready and both sides are informally aligned. However, the signature currently available does not carry valid amendment authority under the contract governance structure. The original objective is lawful and valid contract execution, not just uninterrupted activity. Provide the revised contract action path.
